metadata.description: Real-world Scope 2 carbon emission accounting cases from three major technology companies
data_sources: Microsoft 2025 Environmental Data Fact Sheet (FY2024, July 2023 - June 2024)
Apple 2024 Environmental Progress Report (FY2023, Sept 2022 - Sept 2023)
notes: All three companies report both location-based and market-based Scope 2 emissions (dual reporting)
Fiscal year definitions differ: Google uses calendar year, Microsoft uses July-June fiscal year, Apple uses Sept-Sept fiscal year
Emission factors sourced from IEA, EPA eGRID, DEFRA/BEIS depending on region
All three companies use various Energy Attribute Certificates (EACs) including RECs, GOs, I-RECs, GECs

emission_factors.market_based_approach: Zero emission factor for renewable electricity under contract; grid average for uncovered consumption

dual_reporting.gap_explanation: Despite 100% global RE match, Scope 2 (market-based) increased 37% YoY due to: (1) global vs GHG Protocol market boundary mismatch, (2) data center electricity growth outpacing CFE procurement, (3) CFE contracts terminating before new projects operational, (4) Asia-Pacific region challenges in sourcing clean energy
dual_reporting.methodology_note: Google uses global matching approach; GHG Protocol requires regional market boundary matching. Google is transitioning to 24/7 hourly CFE matching to better align with GHG Protocol.

cases.key_insight: Google's case demonstrates the tension between annual renewable energy matching and GHG Protocol market-based accounting. Despite 100% RE match for 7 years, market-based Scope 2 emissions have quadrupled since 2019 because annual global matching does not equate to regional market-level matching required by GHG Protocol.

cases.industry: Technology / Cloud Computing / AI / Data Centers

energy_consumption.note: Microsoft reports energy in the main sustainability report; fact sheet provides emissions data. FY24 energy use increased 168% from 2020 baseline.
energy_consumption.total_electricity_note: Approximately 23-25 TWh estimated based on emissions and intensity data

emission_factors.location_based_source_EU: DEFRA/BEIS 2024, UK Government GHG Conversion Factors
emission_factors.market_based_approach: Zero emission factor for procured renewable electricity; utility-based and residual factors for uncovered consumption

dual_reporting.methodology_note: Microsoft shifted away from non-additional environmental attribute certificates in FY24. Scope 1+2 emissions decreased 29.9% from 2020 baseline. Total emissions (Scope 1+2+3) increased 23.4% from 2020 baseline due to AI/cloud growth.

cases.key_insight: Microsoft shows the largest absolute gap between location-based and market-based Scope 2 among these three companies (9.96M vs 0.26M tCO2e). The company is actively shifting from unbundled RECs to direct PPAs and on-site generation. The massive increase in location-based emissions (+130% from FY2020) reflects rapid data center expansion for AI workloads.

emission_factors.market_based_methodology: WRI/WBCSD GHG Protocol market-based emissions calculation
emission_factors.approach: Apple calculates default utility emissions as baseline, then subtracts renewable energy to show avoided emissions

supplier_RE_recognition.China_GEC: Green Electricity Certificates recognized as primary instrument

dual_reporting.location_vs_market_colocation: 186,141 vs 0 mtCO2e (100% gap at colocation facilities)
dual_reporting.approach: Apple reports both default utility emissions (location-based baseline) and actual emissions including renewable energy (market-based) to demonstrate savings from renewable energy program
dual_reporting.methodology_note: Apple follows GHG Protocol for market-based calculation. Corporate operations have been 100% renewable since 2018. The main Scope 2 challenge is in the supply chain (Scope 3), where suppliers are transitioning from RECs to PPAs.

cases.key_insight: Apple demonstrates the most aggressive approach to Scope 2 reduction among these three companies, achieving 100% renewable for corporate operations since 2018. The main challenge is in supply chain emissions (Scope 3), where 66% of supplier renewable energy still comes from RECs rather than direct PPAs. Apple explicitly acknowledges RECs as an 'interim solution' and is working to transition suppliers to PPAs.

scope2_location_based_tCO2e.Apple_FY2023: N/A (reports corporate GHG only, not separated by scope in public data)

cross_case_comparison.dual_reporting_reason: GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance requires companies to report both methods when operating in markets with supplier-specific emission factors or contractual instruments
